title: National Food and Nutrition Commission Act, 1967

sections.text: [41 of 1967; 23 of 1975]An Act to establish a body corporate to be known as the National Food and Nutrition Commission; to provide for its membership; to specify its functions; and to provide for matters incidental thereto.

sections.heading: 1. Short title
sections.text: This Act may be cited as the National Food and NutritionCommissionAct.

sections.heading: 2. Interpretation
sections.text: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—"Commission" means the National Food and Nutrition Commission established under section three;"member" means a member of theCommission."Nutrition Group" means a voluntary, nonprofit-making organisation concerned with combatting malnutrition, the constitution whereof has been approved by theCommission.[As amended by ActNo. 23 of 1975]

sections.heading: 3. Establishment of National Food and NutritionCommission
sections.text: There is hereby established a National Food and NutritionCommissionwhich shall have the powers and duties prescribed in this Act.
There is hereby established a National Food and NutritionCommissionwhich shall have the powers and duties prescribed in this Act.
The objects of theCommissionshall be those set out in the Schedule.

TheCommissionshall be a body corporate to be known as the National Food and NutritionCommissionwith perpetual succession and a common seal, with powers to sue and be sued under its corporate name, to enter into contracts, to acquire, purchase and hold all land and personal property whatsoever, whether situate in the Republic or elsewhere, and to grant, devise, let, alienate, sell, mortgage, charge or otherwise dispose of the same and to do all such acts and things as bodies corporate may by law do and as are incidental or appertain to a body corporate.
receive such sums of money by way of grant or donation or in any other way from any source and expend and invest such sums as it may deem expedient;
receive such sums of money by way of grant or donation or in any other way from any source and expend and invest such sums as it may deem expedient;
subject to the approval of the Minister responsible for finance, borrow by way of loan or otherwise, such sums as it may require to meet or discharge its obligations;
with the approval of the Minister, employ a secretary and such other staff as appears to theCommissionto be necessary, on such terms and conditions as theCommissionmay determine.

sections.heading: 4. Membership ofCommission
sections.text: TheCommissionshall consist of five members appointed by the Minister of whom one shall be designated by the Minister as chairman.
TheCommissionshall consist of five members appointed by the Minister of whom one shall be designated by the Minister as chairman.
TheCommissionmay exercise its powers and duties notwithstanding any vacancy in its number.
Amembershall, subject to the provisions of this Act, hold office for two years.
A retiringmembershall be eligible for reappointment.
On the expiration of the period for which amemberis appointed, he shall continue to hold office until his successor has been appointed but in no case shall such further period exceed three months.
hold office on such conditions; and
hold office on such conditions; and
be paid out of the funds of theCommissionsuch remuneration and allowances;

sections.heading: 5. Vacation of office by members
sections.text: upon his death; and
upon his death; and
upon his death; and
if he is absent from two consecutive meetings of theCommission, of which he has had notice, without the permission of the chairman; or
upon the expiry of one month's notice in writing of his intention to resign his office given by him to the Minister; or
if, in the opinion of the Minister, he is mentally or physically incapable of performing his duties as amember; or
if, in the opinion of the Minister, he is guilty of improper conduct and is so notified by the Minister; or
if he is adjudged bankrupt.

When amember's office is vacated, the Minister shall appoint a person to fill the vacancy until the expiration of the period during which suchmemberwould, but for the vacation of his office, have continued in office.

Whenever there are three or more vacancies on theCommission, the Minister may perform all the functions of theCommissionuntil such time as sufficient vacancies have been filled to enable a quorum of theCommissionto be formed.

sections.heading: 6. Procedure ofCommission
sections.text: Subject to the provisions of this Act, theCommissionmay determine its own procedure.
Subject to the provisions of this Act, theCommissionmay determine its own procedure.

TheCommissionshall meet at such times and places as may be necessary or expedient for the transaction of business and such meetings shall be held at such times and places and on such dates as theCommissionmay determine:Provided that the chairman may at any time call a meeting of theCommissionand shall do so on the request in writing made to him in that behalf by any two members.
Three of the members shall be a quorum at any meeting of theCommission.
If the chairman is for any reason absent from any meeting of theCommission, the members present may elect one of their number to preside at such meeting.

Any matter for decision by theCommissionshall be determined by a majority of the members present and voting and, where there is an equality of votes, the chairman or thememberpresiding shall have a casting vote in addition to his deliberative vote.

sections.heading: 7. Special committees
sections.text: TheCommissionmay appoint special committees of experts to advise it on any specified matter.
TheCommissionmay appoint special committees of experts to advise it on any specified matter.
Members may be appointed to special committees who are not members of theCommissionand whose names do not appear on the panel of experts established by the Minister.
There may be paid to amemberof a special committee by theCommissionsuch fees and allowances as the Minister, after consultation with the Minister responsible for finance, may, in his case, fix.
The Minister may establish a panel of experts from which theCommissionmay appoint the members of any special committee.

sections.heading: 8. Funds ofCommission
sections.text: such moneys as may be payable to theCommissionfrom moneys appropriated by Parliament; and
such moneys as may be payable to theCommissionfrom moneys appropriated by Parliament; and
such moneys or assets which may vest in or accrue to theCommissionfrom other sources.

sections.heading: 9. Accounts, audit and reports
sections.text: TheCommissionshall cause to be kept proper books of account which shall be audited annually by auditors approved by the Minister responsible for finance.
TheCommissionshall cause to be kept proper books of account which shall be audited annually by auditors approved by the Minister responsible for finance.
The financial year of theCommissionshall be the calendar year.
TheCommissionshall as soon as may be, and in any case not later than six months after the end of each financial year, prepare a report for the past financial year.
The report required by subsection (3) shall, together with a certified copy of the accounts of theCommissionas audited and the annual report of the auditors, be laid before the National Assembly.

sections.heading: 10. Miscellaneous administrative provisions
sections.text: The seal of theCommissionshall be such as may be determined by theCommission, and the fixing of the seal shall be authenticated by the signatures of any two members.
The seal of theCommissionshall be such as may be determined by theCommission, and the fixing of the seal shall be authenticated by the signatures of any two members.

Any document purporting to be a document executed under the seal of theCommissionauthenticated as aforesaid shall be received in evidence and shall, unless the contrary is proved, be deemed to be so executed.

Any contract or instrument which, if made or executed by a person not being a body corporate, would not be required to be under seal may be made or executed on behalf of theCommissionby any person generally or specially authorised by theCommissionto do so.
No stamp duty, transfer duty or other duty or registration fees shall be payable in respect of any transfer of property to theCommission.

The validity of any proceedings, act or decision of theCommissionshall not be affected by any vacancy in the membership of theCommissionor by any defect in the appointment of anymemberor by reason that any person not entitled so to do took part in the proceedings.

sections.heading: 11. Minister may give directions toCommission
sections.text: The Minister may give to theCommissionsuch general or special directions with respect to the exercise of the powers and duties of theCommissionas the Minister may consider necessary and theCommissionshall comply with all such directions.

The Minister may give to theCommissionsuch general or special directions with respect to the exercise of the powers and duties of theCommissionas the Minister may consider necessary and theCommissionshall comply with all such directions.
TheCommissionshall make regular reports to the Minister on the progress of the national food and nutrition programme.

sections.heading: 12. Nutrition Groups
sections.text: TheCommissionshall keep and maintain in such form as may be prescribed, a register wherein shall be registered, all Nutrition Groups formed in Zambia.
TheCommissionshall keep and maintain in such form as may be prescribed, a register wherein shall be registered, all Nutrition Groups formed in Zambia.

EveryNutrition Groupshall operate in accordance with the directions of theCommission, which shall oversee and guide their activities, and submit a report relating thereto to the Minister, as and when it thinks it desirable, or it is required by the Minister to do so.

ANutrition Groupshall not receive or request for any contribution, financial aid or other assistance from any source within or outside Zambia, except through or with the approval in writing of theCommission.

Any contribution, financial aid or other assistance received by or through theCommissionfor purposes of Nutrition Groups shall be acknowledged by theCommissionby means of an official receipt or acknowledgment in writing.

TheCommissionmay, at any time, with the approval of the Minister, dissolve anyNutrition Group, without assigning any reason therefor, and when anyNutrition Groupis dissolved, its name shall be struck off the register kept and maintained under subsection (1).
For carrying into effect the provisions of this section the Minister may, by statutory instrument, make regulations.[As amended ActNo. 23 of 1995]
To reduce mortality due directly or indirectly to malnutrition in children focus public attention on the nutritional needs of children and youth.
To improve the nutritional status of vulnerable groups (mothers, infants, school and pre-school children).
To create community interest in better nutrition, to arouse public awareness of the serious impact of malnutrition and to instil public confidence in the solutions to the problems.

To reduce the incidence of malnutrition and under-nutrition by providing enough highly nourishing food, at all seasons of the year, to ensure a long, healthy life and diets conducive to maximum intelligence and mental health.
To make provision in relation to nutrition for the rapidly growing population.
To ensure adequacy of diets in institutions.
To provide food consumption and nutrition data on a representative national scale.
To incorporate the concept of improvement of nutrition in food and agricultural development planning.
To take all necessary steps to facilitate the implementation of Government's approved policy in relation to the national food and nutrition programme.
To collate all information already available regarding food and nutrition in Zambia.
To assist in the co-ordination of training in food and nutrition at professional, supervisory and field levels.
To initiate studies relating to food and nutrition, especially those relating to children and youth, and make recommendations on solutions to these problems.
To arrange for execution of work, either directly or through agencies, in relation to subjects relating to food and nutrition which are not part of any ministerial portfolio.
To establish a nutrition library.
To maintain statistical records of national nutrition.
To stimulate public relation activities in relation to the National Food and Nutrition Programme and, in particular, to focus public attention on the nutritional needs of children and youth.
To liaise with International Agencies and friendly Governments regarding aid to the programme subject to the Government's procedures laid down in this connection.
